questions.question: What is the primary function of a camera in image formation?
options: A) To focus light onto a sensor
B) To modify the image
C) To convert light into electrical signal
D) To process the image
questions.explanation: The primary function of a camera is to focus light onto a sensor, which then converts the light into an electrical signal.

questions.question: What type of image is formed when light hits a sensor?
options: A) Photometric image
B) Geometric image
C) Spectral image
D) Time-series image
questions.explanation: A photometric image is formed when light hits a sensor, which measures the intensity of the light and represents it as a numerical value.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a lens in image formation?
options: A) To focus light onto a sensor
B) To modify the image
C) To convert light into electrical signal
D) To process the image
questions.explanation: The purpose of a lens in image formation is to focus light onto a sensor, allowing it to capture the image.

questions.question: What is the main difference between photometric and geometric images?
options: A) Photometric images are more detailed
B) Geometric images are more detailed
C) Photometric images represent light intensity, while geometric images represent spatial location
D) Photometric images are captured using a camera, while geometric images are captured using a 3D scanner
questions.explanation: The main difference between photometric and geometric images is that photometric images represent light intensity, while geometric images represent spatial location.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a sensor in image formation?
options: A) To focus light onto a sensor
B) To modify the image
C) To convert light into electrical signal
D) To process the image
questions.explanation: The purpose of a sensor in image formation is to convert the light into an electrical signal, which is then processed to form an image.
